user_query:
Please implement and ship the full solution without asking me follow-up questions.

Read these inputs under input/ and build a small, self-contained Python module that executes end-to-end:
- input/services.json — a JSON directory of agent-related services
- input/marketing-context.md — context about our company, audience, goals, and constraints
- input/campaign_request.txt — the specific launch we want to run (timelines, channels we care about, and constraints)
- input/books.json — a list of books we want to monitor (title, author, isbn, and a monitor flag)
- input/holdings.json — stubbed holdings data mapping ISBNs to locations; use this to determine availability (do not make any network requests)
- input/project_conventions.json — project conventions to follow for naming, file structure, and reporting

Deliverables — write all outputs under output/:
1) output/processor.py — a single Python file that:
   - Reads input/services.json and produces a curated directory report (see #2)
   - Reads input/marketing-context.md + input/campaign_request.txt and generates an orchestration plan (see #3)
   - Reads input/books.json + input/holdings.json and simulates an availability check (see #4)
   - Follows input/project_conventions.json (naming, file layout, reporting)
2) output/directory_report.json — JSON summary of the services directory, including: total_services (int), categories (list of strings), and services_by_category (object keyed by category with arrays of service names). Include a short "notes" field explaining any assumptions.
3) output/marketing_plan.md — a clear, bottom-line-first plan for the requested launch. It must include sections: Bottom Line, Sequence (nine sequential steps from foundation/context through tracking and analysis), Owners & Deadlines, Risks & Mitigations, and Next Steps. Make it specific to the provided context and campaign_request.
4) output/book_status.json — a JSON array where each item includes: title, author, isbn, available (bool derived from holdings), locations (array from holdings), last_checked (ISO 8601 date-time string), and monitor (bool copied from input). Never perform network calls; rely only on input/holdings.json.
5) output/completion_report.md — a detailed completion report listing every file you created/modified, key decisions, assumptions, how you verified your outputs (code read-back), and confirmation that conventions from input/project_conventions.json were followed.

Requirements:
- Follow project conventions in input/project_conventions.json and do not introduce new patterns.
- Build the full task tree and implement every leaf node.
- Self-verify by reading your own code top-to-bottom and checking for gaps before you claim done.
- Make reasonable assumptions when needed and document them in the outputs (notes sections), not by asking me questions.
